summary: Every business requirement R1–R50 is genuinely addressed by the technical design, not merely cited. I independently checked each requirement against the design body (not just the §11 traceability table): access/landing (R1–R4) via existing root+Devise and page-level empty state; layout/summary (R5–R7) via the 3-panel grid and _summary with zero-shown-not-hidden; active pipelines (R8–R18) via Dashboard::ActivePipelines with the derived attention/last-active logic, phase-progress indicator, non-color-alone status, attention-first cap, and per-row detail links; recent activity (R19–R24) via the 4-source merged RecentActivity with plain-language descriptions, newest-first, per-item links, and the 15-item window; fleet health (R25–R30) via FleetHealth with online/offline counts, per-worker detail, staleness flag, role-coverage-gap warning, and empty state; live updates (R31–R33) via the per-user Dashboard::Broadcast stream + fleet polling + safely/section_error; responsiveness/a11y (R34–R35); and the full open-questions modal flow (R36–R50) via the structured artifact, native <dialog>, client-side compose/validate, and the PhasesController#answers turbo_stream branch. No over-design: the two notable structural additions — the open_questions_structured sibling artifact and removal of the inline _define_panel answers form — both trace to R39/R40 needs and confirmed clarifying-question defaults (Q14) respectively, so nothing is built that no requirement asks for. Only observation (non-blocking, left for Build): the standalone HomeController#fleet_health poll endpoint doesn't wrap its query in `safely`, so a failure on periodic frame reload would surface a raw error rather than the R33 section-error message; R33 is satisfied on initial load, and this is an implementation detail the design's stated intent already covers.
